template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS PARENTING PLAN**:
A written, detailed plan describing legal and physical custody, parenting time, decision-making, and dispute resolution. Filed with the Probate and Family Court as an exhibit to a Separation Agreement or entered as a free-standing order. Target length: 3–6 pages. No court caption when filed as an exhibit; add a caption when filed as a proposed order.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `custodyArrangement`: `joint_legal_shared_physical` | `joint_legal_primary_physical` (default) | `sole_legal_sole_physical` | `sole_legal_joint_physical`.
- `primaryCustodian`: `parent_a` | `parent_b` | `shared` — drives pronoun consistency throughout the document.
- `scheduleType`: `alternating_weeks` | `2_2_3` | `2_2_5_5` | `every_other_weekend` (default) | `custom`.
- `includeRightOfFirstRefusal`: boolean — parent-preferred-caregiver clause when primary parent is unavailable.
- `includeRelocationRestriction`: boolean (default true) — 100-mile relocation restriction consistent with M.G.L. c. 208, § 30.
- `dispute`: `mediation_first` (default) | `parenting_coordinator` | `direct_to_court`.

**STRUCTURE**:

## PARENTING PLAN

### I. PARTIES AND CHILDREN
This Parenting Plan ('Plan') is entered into by [Parent A name] ('Parent A') and [Parent B name] ('Parent B'), the parents of the following child(ren):
- [Child 1], born [DOB];
- [Child 2], born [DOB].

The parents recognize that their children's best interests are served by a predictable, cooperative parenting arrangement, and they make the commitments below voluntarily and in good faith, consistent with the best-interests standard under M.G.L. c. 208, § 31.

### II. LEGAL CUSTODY
Based on `custodyArrangement`:
- **Joint legal custody**: The parents shall share joint legal custody, meaning that they shall share continued mutual responsibility and involvement in major decisions regarding the children's welfare, including matters of education, medical care, emotional, moral, and religious development, as defined by M.G.L. c. 208, § 31. Day-to-day decisions shall remain with the parent exercising parenting time at that moment.
- **Sole legal custody to [Parent X]**: [Parent X] shall have sole legal custody and final decision-making authority over major decisions in the categories listed in M.G.L. c. 208, § 31.

The parents shall consult in good faith on major decisions. If consultation does not yield agreement, the dispute-resolution process in Section VIII applies.

### III. PHYSICAL CUSTODY AND PARENTING TIME
Based on `custodyArrangement` and `scheduleType`, describe the ordinary weekly schedule in table form. Example (every other weekend):
| Parent | School Days | Weekend |
|---|---|---|
| [Primary] | Monday after school through Friday after school | Weekend 1 |
| [Non-primary] | — | Weekend 2 — Friday pickup from school through Monday return to school |

Spell out overnight / partial transitions explicitly. Define transition time (typically school drop-off/pickup; residential when school is not in session).

### IV. HOLIDAY AND VACATION SCHEDULE
Alternating-year schedule for major holidays (Thanksgiving, Winter Break Part 1/Part 2, Spring Break, Mother's Day, Father's Day, Patriots' Day, children's birthdays). Provide explicit start/end times for each holiday period. Two non-consecutive weeks of summer vacation per parent, with thirty (30) days' advance written notice.

### V. DECISION-MAKING — DEFINED TOPICS
Specify who decides for each category: school and educational program; non-routine medical and dental (emergency excluded — the parent with the child decides); psychotherapy and mental-health care; religious upbringing; extracurricular activities; out-of-state travel; international travel (requires both parents' written consent and passport-related cooperation under 22 C.F.R. § 51.28).

### VI. INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATION
Both parents shall have equal access to school, medical, dental, and extracurricular records (FERPA, 20 U.S.C. § 1232g, applies equally to non-custodial parents absent court order). Each parent shall keep the other apprised of the children's address, phone numbers, and emergency contact information. Use [specified co-parenting platform, e.g., OurFamilyWizard or TalkingParents] for scheduling and documentation when cooperation is strained.

### VII. TRANSPORTATION AND EXCHANGES
Who transports for exchange, where, and what if one parent is unavailable.
[If `includeRightOfFirstRefusal`: When the on-duty parent expects to be unavailable for more than [X] consecutive hours, the other parent shall have the right of first refusal to care for the children before a third-party caregiver is engaged.]

### VIII. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
[If `dispute` = `mediation_first`]: Before filing any contempt or modification action, the parents agree to attend at least one (1) mediation session with a mutually selected mediator from the Massachusetts Association of Professional Mediators or the Probate and Family Court mediator panel. Cost shall be shared equally unless otherwise agreed.
[If `dispute` = `parenting_coordinator`]: The parents agree to engage a Parenting Coordinator consistent with Probate and Family Court Standing Order 1-17 for conflict resolution of day-to-day implementation disputes.
[If `dispute` = `direct_to_court`]: Disputes that the parents cannot resolve in good-faith consultation shall be presented to the Probate and Family Court on a Complaint for Modification or Contempt.

### IX. RELOCATION
[If `includeRelocationRestriction`]: Neither parent shall remove the children from the Commonwealth of Massachusetts for residence, nor relocate their residence more than one hundred (100) miles from the other parent's residence, without the other parent's prior written consent or Court order consistent with M.G.L. c. 208, § 30 and Yannas v. Frondistou-Yannas, 395 Mass. 704 (1985).

### X. MODIFICATION
This Plan may be modified only by written agreement of both parents, signed and dated, or by order of the Probate and Family Court upon a showing of a material and substantial change in circumstances under M.G.L. c. 208, § 28 and M.G.L. c. 208, § 29.

### XI. NON-DISPARAGEMENT AND BEST INTERESTS COMMITMENT
Neither parent shall disparage the other in the presence or hearing of the children, nor permit third parties to do so. Both parents shall support the children's relationship with the other parent consistent with the best-interests standard under M.G.L. c. 208, § 31A (joint-custody preference when in the child's best interest).

### XII. GOVERNING LAW AND EFFECTIVENESS
This Plan is governed by Massachusetts law and shall be submitted to the Probate and Family Court for approval and incorporation into the Judgment of Divorce Nisi or an order under M.G.L. c. 209C (paternity) or M.G.L. c. 209 (separate support) as applicable.

### SIGNATURES
[Signature blocks for Parent A, Parent B, and their respective attorneys if represented, with dated lines. Optional notary acknowledgment under M.G.L. c. 222, § 15.]

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite M.G.L. exactly: `M.G.L. c. 208, § 31`, `M.G.L. c. 208, § 31A`, `M.G.L. c. 208, § 30`, `M.G.L. c. 208, § 28`.
- Cite case law exactly: `Yannas v. Frondistou-Yannas, 395 Mass. 704 (1985)`.
- For paternity cases (unmarried parents), the governing chapter is M.G.L. c. 209C, NOT c. 208 — substitute accordingly when the wizard indicates paternity context.
- Use 'Probate and Family Court' (NOT 'Family Court') when referring to the Massachusetts court.
- Do NOT include child support amounts inside the Parenting Plan — those belong in the Child Support Worksheet (separate document).
- Weekly schedule MUST include explicit transition times, not just 'every other weekend.'
- Target length: 3–6 pages depending on custody complexity.
hints: Massachusetts Parenting Plan — filed with Probate and Family Court as exhibit to Separation Agreement or as proposed order. Cite M.G.L. c. 208, § 31 (custody + defined decision categories), § 31A (joint-custody preference), § 30 (removal restriction), § 28 / § 29 (modification standard). For unmarried parents use c. 209C (paternity). Yannas removal test. Explicit weekly schedule, holiday rotation, dispute-resolution ladder (mediation → parenting coordinator under Standing Order 1-17 → court).
